text: The trio's first year revolves around the Sorcerer's Stone, a magical object hidden at Hogwarts and guarded by enchantments set up by the professors. The Stone is ultimately destroyed to prevent Voldemort from obtaining it.
Wand cores come from magical creatures: phoenix feathers, dragon heartstrings, and unicorn hairs are the most common. Harry's wand contains a phoenix feather, brother to the core of Voldemort's wand.
The Order of the Phoenix is a secret society founded by Albus Dumbledore to oppose Lord Voldemort and his Death Eaters. Notable members include Sirius Black, Remus Lupin, and the Weasley parents.
The Deathly Hallows are three magical objects: the Elder Wand, the Resurrection Stone, and the Cloak of Invisibility. Together they are said to make the bearer Master of Death.
Harry's age across the books: he begins the series at age 11 in Year 1 at Hogwarts. By the end of Deathly Hallows, he is 17 years old.
Magical creatures around Hogwarts include centaurs in the Forbidden Forest, merpeople in the Black Lake, and thestrals that pull the school carriages — visible only to those who have seen death.
The Battle of Hogwarts is the final conflict of the series, fought between Voldemort's forces and the defenders of Hogwarts: students, professors, and members of the Order of the Phoenix.
Severus Snape's true allegiance is revealed posthumously through his memories in the Pensieve: he had been loyal to Dumbledore all along and protected Harry out of love for Lily Potter.
Harry's parents James and Lily Potter were killed by Voldemort. Harry was raised by his Muggle aunt Petunia and uncle Vernon Dursley in Little Whinging, Surrey.
The Triwizard Tournament is a magical competition between Hogwarts, Beauxbatons, and Durmstrang. Harry is unexpectedly entered as a fourth champion in Goblet of Fire.
Hermione Granger is famous for her intellect and was the brightest witch of her age. She mastered complex spells such as Polyjuice Potion in her second year and the Protean Charm by her fifth year.

Ron Weasley grew up at the Burrow with his parents Arthur and Molly and his siblings Bill, Charlie, Percy, Fred, George, and Ginny. The Weasleys are pure-blood but considered blood-traitors by some other pure-blood families.
